response: Originating in the 1960s as a US Defense Department initiative, the internet evolved from the 1969 ARPANET demonstration through critical technical milestones like the 1983 adoption of TCP/IP and Tim Berners-Lee’s 1989 invention of the World Wide Web. Following the volatile dot-com investment boom and 2001 market crash, the network achieved mainstream ubiquity through early web browsers, mid-2000s social media platforms, and the 2007 smartphone revolution. Today, the internet connects over 5 billion people globally, having transformed from a limited academic and military experiment into the indispensable infrastructure underpinning modern commerce, communication, and daily life.
